---
title: "Pull multiple sensors using parallel processing"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
author: Liz Roten and Nicole Sullivan, `r Sys.Date()`
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Pull multiple sensors using parallel processing}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  \usepackage[utf8]{inputenc}
---
```{r setup, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  eval = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  cache = TRUE
)

options(scipen = 9999)
```

## Pull data for select sensors for all 2019  

### Using `{doParallel}` and `{foreach}`

```{r}
library(tc.sensors)
library(doParallel)
library(foreach)
library(data.table)
library(tictoc)

sensor_config <- pull_configuration()
sensor_ids <- pull_sensor_ids()

cores <- detectCores()
cl <- makeCluster(cores)
registerDoParallel(cl)
tic() # Start the timer

foreach(j = sensor_ids[[1]][633:714]) %dopar% {
  date_range <- seq(as.Date("2019/01/01"), as.Date("2019/12/31"), by = "days")
  n <- length(date_range)
  loops_ls <- vector("list", n)

  for (i in 1:n) {
    loops_ls[[i]] <- tc.sensors::pull_sensor(j, date_range[[i]])
  }

  loops_df <- data.table::rbindlist(loops_ls)
  data.table::fwrite(loops_df, paste0("../../MnDOT_Loop_Detectors/Volume-Occupancy/2019/Sensor ", j, ".csv"))
}
toc()
stopCluster(cl)

# > 215.58 sec elapsed
```

### Using `{furrr}`

```{r}
library(purrr)
library(furrr)

sensor_config <- pull_configuration()
sensor_ids <- pull_sensor_ids()

date_range <- seq(as.Date("2019/01/01"), as.Date("2019/12/31"), by = "days")

plan(multiprocess)

tic() # Start the timer
sensor_data <- future_map(sensor_ids[[1]][633:714],
  .f = function(det_name) {
    future_map_dfr(seq(1:length(date_range)), .f = function(x) {
      tc.sensors::pull_sensor(det_name, date_range[[x]])
    })
  }
)
toc()

# > 225.02 sec elapsed
```


## Save to Microsoft Access Database  

```{r}
library(RODBC)

test_conn <- odbcConnectAccess2007("../LoopDBTest.accdb")
sqlTables(test_conn, tableType = "TABLE")$TABLE_NAME # Check available tables

sqlSave(
  channel = test_conn,
  dat = loops_df,
  append = T,
  tablename = "testtable",
  rownames = F
) # Prevents RStudio from crashing

check <- RODBC::sqlQuery(test_conn, "SELECT * from testtable WHERE date = 20191231")
```


<a href="https://metrocouncil.org" target="_blank"><img src="../man/figures/main-logo.png" style="margin-left: 50%;margin-right: 50%;"><div></div></a>
